turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having this persistent cough for about two weeks now, and it's really been affecting my breathing. I've tried taking over-the-counter medications to help with it, but they haven't been giving me much relief.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: It started out just being a dry cough, but then it turned into a productive cough and I've been bringing up yellowish-green mucus. It's really frustrating because even when I'm not coughing, I still feel like there's something stuck in my throat and it's hard to breathe deeply.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: To be honest, yeah... sometimes I get a little short of breath and I'll break out in a sweat. It's not just my chest, it's more like all over, and it's kind of scary because I'm worried that something might be really wrong with me.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: No, not usually... but sometimes at night, I'll wake up soaked through my pajamas. It's like my body just overheats and then cools down suddenly, leaving me all sweaty.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: Actually, yes... there is some sweating. It usually happens in the evenings or when I'm lying down, and it's more like a low-grade fever feeling, around 100 degrees Fahrenheit or so. The sweat doesn't really start until I've been experiencing symptoms for a while, but it's definitely something that's happened consistently over the past week or two.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: The discomfort is mainly centered on my right side. It's a sharp pain that gets worse when I breathe deeply or cough. Sometimes it feels like it's spreading to other areas of my chest, but it's always most intense on the right side.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: No, it doesn't really radiate to any other areas. The pain is pretty localized to my right chest and shoulder area. Sometimes I'll get a bit of discomfort in my arm on that side if I'm coughing or deep breathing, but it's not a sharp pain like the one in my chest.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: No, there's no tingling or numbness. It's just the sharp pain and discomfort that makes me wince when I breathe deeply or cough. And to be honest, it's been really affecting my daily life - I've had to take time off work because of it and even simple tasks like walking up stairs feel exhausting.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: To be honest, no... nothing out of the ordinary in my arms or legs. It's all pretty much centered around my chest and breathing issues. And yeah, just the fatigue from it all has been really getting to me - I've missed a few days of work and even simple activities like exercise or walking feel like they're going to exhaust me.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: No, I haven't noticed any swelling in my ankles, feet, or hands. But to be honest, the fatigue is really getting to me - even simple tasks like cooking dinner feel like a chore because I'm so tired all the time.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: No, my shoes don't feel tighter and I haven't noticed any puffiness in my legs. To be honest, I'm just really worried about what's going on with my body right now - this cough and breathing stuff is really scaring me.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: Actually, yeah... sometimes when I'm experiencing symptoms, I'll get a bit of nausea or queasiness. It's not always there, but it can be pretty unpleasant. And to be honest, my appetite has been really off too - I've been feeling kind of sick to my stomach even when I try to eat.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: Yes... it's usually after the symptoms have started, but sometimes I'll get a little queasy before they hit. It's like my body is just feeling really off and nauseous all over, not just in one spot. And yeah, that's also made eating feel pretty unpleasant lately.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: I've had a low-grade fever, around 100.8 degrees Fahrenheit, and I get these night sweats where I wake up soaking through my pajamas. It's like my body just overheats and then cools down suddenly, leaving me all sweaty.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: My appetite has definitely been off lately. I've been feeling kind of sick to my stomach even when I try to eat, so I haven't been eating as much as usual. And to be honest, I don't think I've lost any weight, but I'm not really sure - it's hard to tell when you're already feeling pretty crummy all the time.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient:
I'm a teacher, so my days are pretty packed. I work with young children every day, and that can be exhausting on its own. As for exercise, I try to get in a walk or some yoga during my lunch break when I can. But lately, it's been really hard to motivate myself because of how tired I've been feeling. My diet is pretty standard - I eat a lot of fruits and vegetables, but I have been relying on takeout and delivery more often than usual since cooking has felt like too much effort.

Also, I should mention that my partner has had a mild cold recently, so we're trying to avoid getting each other sick. We've been taking precautions like washing our hands frequently and using hand sanitizer.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I try to stay somewhat active, but it's been really tough lately with how tired I am feeling all the time. I used to be more into yoga and walking, but even just doing a few poses or taking a short walk feels like too much effort for me right now. I'm hoping that once this cough and breathing stuff gets sorted out, I'll be able to get back into my usual routine.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient:
Honestly, it's been really hard to keep up with my normal eating habits lately. Like I said, cooking feels like too much effort, so I've been relying on takeout and delivery more often than usual. Sometimes I'll just grab something quick from a café or restaurant near work because I don't have the energy to cook at home. My diet is probably not as healthy as it usually is when I'm feeling better.

(By the way, I should mention that my mother has asthma, but I've never really been concerned about that before... and my maternal grandmother passed away from lung cancer a few years ago, but I don't know if that's relevant to what's going on with me right now.)

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, which is probably why I've always been more aware of my own breathing and lungs. And yeah, my grandmother passed away from lung cancer a few years ago, but like I said, I don't know if that's relevant to what's going on with me right now.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient:
I've been trying to think about whether there are any other medical issues in my family, and it seems like my grandmother's lung cancer could be a factor. She passed away when I was still pretty young, but my mom has always been really open with me about her health and I'm sure she'd want me to share this information if it might help figure out what's going on with me.

Also, I should mention that several students in my class have had upper respiratory infections recently, so I've been a bit worried that maybe I caught something from them. But like I said, I've tried taking OTC medications and they haven't really helped, which is why I'm here seeing you today.
